reasoning: The informal statement claims that any abelian simple group G is isomorphic to Z_p for some prime p, equivalently: G is cyclic of prime order. The Lean conclusion `IsCyclic G ∧ ∃ G_fin : Fintype G, Nat.Prime (@card G G_fin)` captures exactly this: it asserts G is cyclic and admits a Fintype structure (i.e., G is finite) whose cardinality is prime. A cyclic group of prime order is isomorphic to ZMod p, and conversely. The hypotheses `CommGroup G` and `IsSimpleGroup G` directly match "abelian simple group", and finiteness is not assumed (it is derived as part of the conclusion through the existential Fintype), faithfully matching the parenthetical "do not assume G is a finite group". So the two statements are logically equivalent.
